title: A Job Interview
summary: An interviewer and an applicant discuss the applicant's background, skills, and motivations during a job interview at a marketing company.

body.speaker: Interviewer
body.text: Good morning. Please come in and take a seat.

body.speaker: Applicant
body.text: Good morning. Thank you for having me.

body.speaker: Interviewer
body.text: So, I've had a chance to read through your CV, and I'm quite impressed. Can you start by telling me a little about yourself?

body.speaker: Applicant
body.text: Of course. I graduated from university three years ago with a degree in marketing. Since then, I've been working at a digital agency where I managed social media accounts for several clients.

body.speaker: Interviewer
body.text: That sounds like relevant experience. What specific skills have you developed in that role?

body.speaker: Applicant
body.text: I've become quite confident with data analytics, content planning, and running paid advertising campaigns. I've also improved my ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines.

body.speaker: Interviewer
body.text: Good. Now, why are you looking to leave your current position?

body.speaker: Applicant
body.text: I've learned a great deal in my current job, and I'm grateful for the experience. However, I feel I've reached a point where I'm ready for a new challenge. This role at your company really excites me because it offers the chance to work on larger campaigns and develop my skills further.

body.speaker: Interviewer
body.text: That's a good answer. Can you give me an example of a time you dealt with a difficult situation at work?

body.speaker: Applicant
body.text: Certainly. Last year, one of our biggest clients was unhappy with the results of a campaign we had run. I arranged a meeting with the client, listened carefully to their concerns, and put together a revised strategy within two days. The client was much happier with the outcome and decided to extend their contract with us.

body.speaker: Interviewer
body.text: Impressive. It sounds like you handle pressure well. What would you say is your greatest weakness?

body.speaker: Applicant
body.text: I think I sometimes take on too much at once because I want to do everything to a high standard. I've been working on this by learning to prioritise tasks and delegate when appropriate.

body.speaker: Interviewer
body.text: That's a very self-aware answer. Do you have any questions for us?

body.speaker: Applicant
body.text: Yes, actually. Could you tell me more about the team I would be working with and the main projects coming up in the next six months?

body.speaker: Interviewer
body.text: Of course. You'd be joining a team of eight people, and we have two major product launches planned for the autumn. There's a lot of exciting work ahead. We'll be in touch by the end of this week to let you know our decision.

body.speaker: Applicant
body.text: That sounds wonderful. Thank you so much for your time. I look forward to hearing from you.

annotations.meaningVi: đã có cơ hội để, đã kịp
annotations.exampleEn: I've had a chance to review your proposal and it looks very promising.

annotations.meaningVi: kinh nghiệm phù hợp, kinh nghiệm liên quan
annotations.exampleEn: Candidates with relevant experience will be given priority.
Data analytics has become an essential skill in most marketing roles.
She has proved she can work under pressure and still deliver quality results.
Working in journalism means constantly meeting tight deadlines.

annotations.meaningVi: sẵn sàng đón nhận thử thách mới
annotations.exampleEn: After five years in the same role, she felt ready for a new challenge.

annotations.meaningVi: đã giải quyết, đã xử lý
annotations.exampleEn: He dealt with the complaint calmly and professionally.
We need to put together a presentation for the board meeting.

annotations.meaningVi: chiến lược đã được điều chỉnh, kế hoạch sửa đổi
annotations.exampleEn: The team came up with a revised strategy after reviewing the initial results.
The company decided to extend their contract with the supplier for another year.
Interviewers often ask about your greatest weakness to see how self-aware you are.

annotations.meaningVi: theo tiêu chuẩn cao, đạt chất lượng tốt
annotations.exampleEn: She always completes her assignments to a high standard.

annotations.meaningVi: ưu tiên công việc, sắp xếp thứ tự ưu tiên
annotations.exampleEn: Learning to prioritise tasks is essential when managing a busy workload.

annotations.meaningVi: phân công, giao việc cho người khác
annotations.exampleEn: A good manager knows when to delegate tasks to their team.

annotations.meaningVi: có ý thức về bản thân, tự nhận thức
annotations.exampleEn: Being self-aware helps you identify areas where you need to improve.
We'll be in touch as soon as we've made a decision.

grammarNotes.title: Present Perfect — Describing experience and ongoing relevance
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Trong đoạn hội thoại, thì hiện tại hoàn thành được dùng nhiều để nói về kinh nghiệm và kỹ năng đã tích lũy đến hiện tại. Ví dụ: "I've been working at a digital agency", "I've become quite confident", "I've also improved my ability". Cấu trúc: have/has + past participle. Thì này nhấn mạnh rằng kinh nghiệm vẫn còn liên quan đến hiện tại, rất phổ biến trong các buổi phỏng vấn xin việc.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The present perfect is used extensively in job interviews to describe accumulated experience that is still relevant today. 'I've been working' (present perfect continuous) emphasises that the activity is ongoing, while 'I've become confident' and 'I've improved' describe completed changes with present relevance. This tense is essential for discussing professional backgrounds.

grammarNotes.title: Past Perfect — Narrating events in sequence
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Thì quá khứ hoàn thành được dùng để mô tả hành động xảy ra trước một hành động khác trong quá khứ. Ví dụ: "one of our biggest clients was unhappy with the results of a campaign we had run". Cấu trúc: had + past participle. Ở đây, "had run" xảy ra trước "was unhappy". Thì này thường xuất hiện khi kể chuyện hoặc mô tả tình huống theo thứ tự thời gian.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The past perfect ('had run') is used to show that one past action occurred before another past action ('was unhappy'). This sequencing is important when recounting professional situations in interviews. The past perfect clarifies the order of events without needing time expressions.

translationVi:
Người phỏng vấn: Chào buổi sáng. Mời vào và ngồi xuống.
Người xin việc: Chào buổi sáng. Cảm ơn vì đã tiếp đón tôi.
Người phỏng vấn: Vậy, tôi đã có dịp xem qua CV của bạn và khá ấn tượng. Bạn có thể bắt đầu bằng cách giới thiệu một chút về bản thân không?
Người xin việc: Tất nhiên. Tôi tốt nghiệp đại học ba năm trước với bằng marketing. Kể từ đó, tôi đã làm việc tại một công ty truyền thông kỹ thuật số, nơi tôi quản lý các tài khoản mạng xã hội cho nhiều khách hàng.
Người phỏng vấn: Nghe có vẻ là kinh nghiệm phù hợp. Bạn đã phát triển những kỹ năng cụ thể nào trong vai trò đó?
Người xin việc: Tôi đã trở nên khá tự tin với phân tích dữ liệu, lập kế hoạch nội dung và chạy các chiến dịch quảng cáo trả phí. Tôi cũng đã cải thiện khả năng làm việc dưới áp lực và đáp ứng deadline gấp.
Người phỏng vấn: Tốt. Bây giờ, tại sao bạn muốn rời vị trí hiện tại?
Người xin việc: Tôi đã học được rất nhiều trong công việc hiện tại và rất biết ơn vì kinh nghiệm này. Tuy nhiên, tôi cảm thấy mình đã đến lúc sẵn sàng cho một thử thách mới. Vị trí này tại công ty của bạn thực sự khiến tôi hứng khởi vì nó mang đến cơ hội làm việc với các chiến dịch lớn hơn và phát triển kỹ năng xa hơn.
Người phỏng vấn: Câu trả lời hay đấy. Bạn có thể cho tôi một ví dụ về lần bạn xử lý tình huống khó khăn tại nơi làm việc không?
Người xin việc: Chắc chắn rồi. Năm ngoái, một trong những khách hàng lớn nhất của chúng tôi không hài lòng với kết quả của chiến dịch chúng tôi đã thực hiện. Tôi đã sắp xếp một cuộc họp với khách hàng, lắng nghe cẩn thận các mối lo ngại của họ, và đưa ra một chiến lược sửa đổi trong vòng hai ngày. Khách hàng hài lòng hơn nhiều với kết quả và quyết định gia hạn hợp đồng với chúng tôi.
Người phỏng vấn: Ấn tượng đấy. Có vẻ bạn xử lý áp lực rất tốt. Bạn cho rằng điểm yếu lớn nhất của bạn là gì?
Người xin việc: Tôi nghĩ đôi khi tôi nhận quá nhiều việc cùng một lúc vì muốn làm mọi thứ theo tiêu chuẩn cao. Tôi đang cải thiện điều này bằng cách học cách ưu tiên công việc và phân công khi thích hợp.
Người phỏng vấn: Đó là câu trả lời rất tự nhận thức. Bạn có câu hỏi nào dành cho chúng tôi không?
Người xin việc: Có, thực ra có. Bạn có thể cho tôi biết thêm về đội ngũ tôi sẽ làm việc cùng và các dự án chính trong sáu tháng tới không?
Người phỏng vấn: Tất nhiên. Bạn sẽ gia nhập một nhóm tám người, và chúng tôi có hai đợt ra mắt sản phẩm lớn được lên kế hoạch vào mùa thu. Sẽ có rất nhiều công việc thú vị phía trước. Chúng tôi sẽ liên lạc với bạn trước cuối tuần này để thông báo quyết định.
Người xin việc: Nghe tuyệt vời quá. Cảm ơn bạn rất nhiều vì thời gian của bạn. Tôi mong nhận được tin từ bạn.

questions.prompt: How long has the applicant been working since graduating?
questions.explanation: The applicant says 'I graduated from university three years ago.'
questions.hint: Listen for when the applicant graduated.

questions.prompt: What type of company does the applicant currently work for?
questions.explanation: The applicant says they have been 'working at a digital agency.'
questions.hint: Think about what kind of company manages social media accounts.

questions.prompt: Which skill does the applicant NOT specifically mention?
questions.explanation: The applicant mentions data analytics, content planning, and paid advertising campaigns, but never mentions video production.
questions.hint: Think about which skill is not part of typical social media management.

questions.prompt: Why is the applicant looking to leave their current job?
options: They had a disagreement with their manager
They feel ready for a new challenge with larger campaigns
Their current salary is too low
questions.explanation: The applicant says they feel 'ready for a new challenge' and this role offers 'the chance to work on larger campaigns.'
questions.hint: The applicant is careful to speak positively about their current employer.

questions.prompt: What problem did the applicant solve in the difficult situation they described?
options: A colleague who refused to cooperate on a project
A technical failure that delayed a product launch
A client who was unhappy with campaign results
A budget cut that affected their team
questions.explanation: The applicant describes a situation where 'one of our biggest clients was unhappy with the results of a campaign we had run.'
questions.hint: Think about who was dissatisfied and why.

questions.prompt: How quickly did the applicant produce a revised strategy for the unhappy client?
questions.explanation: The applicant says they 'put together a revised strategy within two days.'
questions.hint: The applicant mentions a specific timeframe.

questions.prompt: What was the outcome of the applicant's revised strategy?
options: The client decided to end their contract
The client was unhappy but agreed to wait for better results
The client was much happier and extended their contract
The client switched to a different agency
questions.explanation: The applicant says 'The client was much happier with the outcome and decided to extend their contract with us.'
questions.hint: Did the client stay or leave after the new strategy?

questions.prompt: What does the applicant identify as their greatest weakness?
options: Taking on too much at once because they want high-quality results
Lack of experience with data tools
questions.explanation: The applicant says 'I sometimes take on too much at once because I want to do everything to a high standard.'
questions.hint: The applicant frames their weakness in a positive light.

questions.prompt: How is the applicant working to improve their weakness?
options: By working longer hours to finish everything
By learning to prioritise tasks and delegate
By asking their manager for feedback regularly
questions.explanation: The applicant says they have been 'learning to prioritise tasks and delegate when appropriate.'
questions.hint: Two strategies are mentioned — think about managing workload.

questions.prompt: What does the applicant ask the interviewer about?
options: The team and upcoming projects in the next six months
questions.explanation: The applicant asks to hear 'more about the team I would be working with and the main projects coming up in the next six months.'
questions.hint: Asking good questions at the end of an interview shows genuine interest.

questions.prompt: When will the interviewer contact the applicant with a decision?
options: By the end of the week
questions.explanation: The interviewer says 'We'll be in touch by the end of this week to let you know our decision.'
questions.hint: Listen for the interviewer's final statement about the timeline.

cloze.template: Interviewer: So, I've had a chance to read through your CV, and I'm quite impressed. Can you start by telling me a little about yourself? Applicant: Of course. I graduated from university three years ago with a degree in marketing. Since then, I've been working at a digital agency where I managed social media accounts for several clients. Interviewer: That sounds like {{b1}} experience. What specific skills have you developed in that role? Applicant: I've become quite confident with data analytics, content planning, and running paid advertising campaigns. I've also improved my ability to work under {{b2}} and meet tight {{b3}}. Interviewer: Good. Now, why are you looking to leave your current position? Applicant: I've learned a great deal in my current job, and I'm grateful for the experience. However, I feel I've reached a point where I'm ready for a new {{b4}}. This role at your company really excites me because it offers the chance to work on larger campaigns and develop my skills further. Interviewer: Can you give me an example of a time you {{b5}} with a difficult situation at work? Applicant: Last year, one of our biggest clients was unhappy with the results of a campaign we had run. I arranged a meeting with the client, listened carefully to their concerns, and put together a {{b6}} strategy within two days. Interviewer: What would you say is your greatest {{b7}}? Applicant: I think I sometimes take on too much at once because I want to do everything to a high {{b8}}. I've been working on this by learning to {{b9}} tasks and delegate when appropriate. Interviewer: Do you have any questions for us? Applicant: Could you tell me more about the team I would be working with and the main projects coming up in the next six months? Interviewer: We'll be {{b10}} by the end of this week to let you know our decision.

blanks.explanation: 'Relevant experience' means experience that is directly related to and useful for the job being applied for.
'Work under pressure' is a fixed collocation meaning to perform well even when there are demanding conditions or tight time constraints.
'Meet tight deadlines' means to complete work by strict time limits — a very common expression in professional contexts.
'Ready for a new challenge' is a standard phrase used in interviews to explain why someone wants to change jobs without criticising their current employer.
'Dealt with a difficult situation' uses the past form of 'deal with,' meaning to handle or manage a problem.
A 'revised strategy' is one that has been changed and improved in response to feedback or new information.
'Greatest weakness' is a standard interview question phrase asking candidates to reflect on areas where they need to improve.
'To a high standard' is a fixed phrase meaning to do something with a high level of quality and care.
'Prioritise tasks' means to decide which tasks are most important and tackle them first — a key professional skill.
'Be in touch' is a phrasal expression meaning to contact someone — here used to tell the candidate when they will hear back.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The applicant frames their weakness — taking on too much — as a positive quality in disguise. Do you think it is honest to present weaknesses this way in a job interview? What are the advantages and risks of this approach for both the applicant and the employer?
